---
title: "Series Overview"
---

<img src="content/images/logo_lwg-long.png" alt="Logo for LegacyWorks Group" width="30%" align="right"/>

The LegacyWorks Group Community of Practice is a space for developing the core competencies needed to show up in service of collaborative, community-driven change initiatives. The Inclusive and Effective Group Facilitation series presents a structured framework for designing and leading collaborative sessions that maximize group potential. Participants will move from foundational design principles to advanced intervention strategies, focusing on both the technical and interpersonal aspects of effective facilitation. 


<br>


:::{.callout-tip icon="false" collapse="false"}
## {{< fa brain >}} Learning Objectives

Through a blend of theory and hands-on practice, participants will learn to:

- **Design for Impact:** Move from simple agendas to inclusive gatherings that foster deep engagement and psychological safety, whether in-person or virtual.
- **Navigate Group Dynamics:** Gain the "facilitator’s eye" to analyze interactions in real-time, identifying how to unlock stuck energy and shift from friction to flow.
- **Master the Decision Pipeline:** Lead groups through the critical stages of divergent, emergent, and convergent thinking to reach decisions with genuine buy-in.
- **Deepen Presence:** Refine your active listening and synthesis skills to help groups discern common ground and synthesize complex ideas into actionable outcomes.

:::

## Course Structure

:::{.panel-tabset}
### 2026-27 Structure

{{< fa person-digging >}} Under construction; check back later

:::

## Course Scheduling

:::{.panel-tabset}
### 2026-27 Schedule

| **Date** | **Series** | **Primary Instructor** |
|:-----:|:-----------------|:---------|
| May 7, 2026 | 1. Facilitation Tools & Practices | <span style="color:#4AB2CB">{{< fa people-group >}} Carrie Kappel </span> |
| June 4, 2026 | 2. Facilitating Access, Inclusion, and Belonging | <span style="color:#4AB2CB">{{< fa people-group >}} Carrie Kappel </span> |
| July 9, 2026 | 3. Supporting Clear and Effective Group Decision Making | <span style="color:#4AB2CB">{{< fa people-group >}} Carrie Kappel </span> |

:::

## Course Policies

{{< fa person-digging >}} Under construction; check back later
